title: dipterocarpus condorensis subsp. penangianus
intro: Dipterocarpus condorensis subsp. penangianus, also known as the Penang dipterocarp, is a subspecies of Dipterocarpus condorensis, a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dipterocarpus condorensis subsp. penangianus are said to include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
